what_it_does: This file documents the conventions for building workspace zone pages inside the studio shell: page registration, required anatomy, status rendering, design-system workspace behavior, embedded guided streams, the init wizard, and layout/token rules.
observations: Each workspace page is one file exporting window.XWorkspace.
Pages must render a .ws-head with Eyebrow and h1.ws-head__title, optionally .ws-head__actions.
All domain state must come from window.MS_DATA; local mock data is prohibited.
Status must use Chip kind or MS_STATUS, never inline status colors.
New pages are registered by adding entries to MS_DATA.zonePages; no extra router wiring is needed beyond the script tag.
decisions.jsx surfaces system-wide design choices from MS_DECISIONS and writes a single underlying tweak via props.setTweak.
New design choices belong in src/decisions.js mapped to a tweak key, not inside decisions.jsx.
StreamHost embeds guided streams bound to a page via MS_STREAMS.forSurface(pageId).
Stream steps commit to real stores: setTweak, MS_ARTIFACTS.create, MS_MEMORY.learn, MS_CONTEXT.addSource.
To offer a stream, add the page id to the stream's surfaces array in src/streams.js.
The init stream uses the same StreamRunner as embedded streams, hosted by WizardScreen in src/screens/wizard.jsx.
ask steps render QuestionBody; synthesize steps render exported Step components driving MS_SYNTH_AI.
The runner builds a brief via MS_STREAMS.briefFromAnswers and calls onComplete(brief) at the ready step.
Embedded streams omit onComplete and fullscreen; Finish just closes.
Multi-column layouts use CSS grid with var(--s-6) gutters.
Dense grids must collapse responsively using styles/responsive.css.
Sizing must use tokens: var(--s-*), var(--r-*), var(--fs-*), MS_MOTION; raw hex/px/ms are forbidden.
standouts: Pages are registered purely through MS_DATA.zonePages plus a script tag, not a conventional router.
decisions.jsx is restricted to a single tweak write and a separate decisions catalog.
The same StreamRunner powers both embedded page streams and the fullscreen init wizard.
conventions: Follows a strict one-file-per-page component contract.
Mandates global window.* data sources and store APIs.
Requires design tokens for all sizing, radii, typography, and motion.
Forbids inline status colors and local mock data.
notes: This is the workspace-page rulebook: it tells developers exactly how to add a new shell page and how to wire it to streams, status, and design tokens.
questions: What is the exact schema of an MS_DATA.zonePages entry?
What value/type is the selection prop passed to StreamHost?
How is MS_LAUNCH resolved and what does it contain?
What are the precise step kinds and their required payloads in src/streams.js?
How does styles/responsive.css handle uncommon grid-template patterns?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define workspace-page conventions, and the content does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth via window globals
Convention over configuration for page registration
No duplicate stores: one tweak, one runner, one status system
intention_signals: It is trying to make workspace pages uniform, self-registering, stateless with respect to mock data, and cleanly integrated with a shared stream runner and design-token system.
novelty: The registration model is unusual: pages are added to a global data array and loaded by script tag rather than through an explicit route configuration.
contribution: Provides a concrete contract for workspace pages, the rules for registering them in the shell, and the integration points for embedded streams and the init wizard.
summary_for_embedding: AGENTS.md for src/workspaces defines the anatomy, registration, state sourcing, status rendering, stream embedding, init wizard reuse, and token-based layout rules for studio shell workspace pages.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to standardize how new workspace zone pages are created and how they connect to global data, status chips, design tweaks, and guided streams.
